KOSPI daily change -7.24% — EXTREME. Circuit breakers triggered per source agent. Driven by dual shock: NK KN-25 MLRS deployment to DMZ (event sig_2026-03-03_018) plus global risk-off from Iran crisis. This is one of the largest single-day KOSPI declines since the 2020 COVID crash. Plausible given the event context but requires UI FLAG and human awareness. Integrating as-is with FLAG status.

Brent crude at $83/bbl — source agent notes +7.23% move. Anomaly flag set by source. Attributed to Strait of Hormuz closure (sig_2026-03-03_002) blocking ~20% of global oil trade. Single-week move of >7% is extreme (2-sigma+ event) but causally explained by simultaneous Hormuz + Bab-el-Mandeb closures (sig_2026-03-03_012). Integrating with FLAG status for UI display. Value is plausible; crisis-driven.

WTI crude at $75/bbl — implied +8.01% weekly move per task brief. While source agent did not flag this entry (anomaly_flag: false), the magnitude is consistent with a >7% weekly move that warrants FLAG status under validation rules. Brent-WTI spread of $8 is elevated but plausible given Hormuz closure impacting Middle Eastern grades more directly. Integrating with FLAG status.
Henry Hub natural gas at $4.12/MMBtu — within normal range. US domestic market relatively insulated from Hormuz-linked LNG disruptions in short term.

Gold at $5155/troy oz — all-time historical high above $5000 threshold. Anomaly flag set by source agent. Driven by: (1) safe-haven demand from Iran crisis, (2) US dollar uncertainty from Section 122 tariff disruptions, (3) continued central bank accumulation trend. While unprecedented in nominal terms, the causal chain is coherent and multi-source confirmed (sig_2026-03-03_007). ACCEPT_WITH_NOTE: value is remarkable but consistent with observed geopolitical + monetary environment. Human reviewer should confirm this is not a data error (e.g., price feed unit mismatch).

Silver at $82/troy oz — multi-decade high per source agent (anomaly flagged). Historically elevated but not unprecedented in real terms (silver reached ~$50 in 1980, ~$50 in 2011). At $82 in a high-inflation environment the nominal high is plausible. Safe-haven demand plus industrial demand from defense sector surge (European spending) supports this level. ACCEPT_WITH_NOTE: human reviewer should verify unit is USD/troy oz.
LME copper at $12987/t — elevated but consistent with supply chain disruptions and infrastructure/defense demand.

ESCALATION REQUIRED. Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei confirmed killed in US-Israeli strikes Feb 28. Interim 3-person council formed pending Assembly of Experts succession process. This is the most consequential single leadership change in the model. Source confidence from agent_07 is 0.88 (pending further confirmation), but agent_08 cross-validation against multiple independent news sources (NPR, Al Jazeera, CNN, Washington Post, CNBC, Iranian state media relay) raises effective confidence to 0.98. The change is confirmed. Escalating for human decision authority given transformative geopolitical significance.

ESCALATION REQUIRED. Leadership transition risk elevated to 'critical' — directly triggered by upd_110 (Khamenei death). The succession process via Assembly of Experts is unprecedented under the current Islamic Republic constitutional framework; no living precedent exists for Supreme Leader succession since Khomeini's death in 1989 (which occurred in more stable conditions). IRGC factionalism, competing clerical claims, and ongoing US-Israeli military pressure create maximum uncertainty. Escalating as derivative of upd_110 escalation.

validated_updates.new_value: theocratic republic under interim emergency governance
validated_updates.notes: ESCALATION REQUIRED. Regime type annotation updated to reflect emergency governance. Core structure (theocratic republic) is unchanged but the qualifier 'under interim emergency governance' is factually accurate and consequential for model users. Source agent confidence is 0.82; alternative classification 'theocratic republic in transition' (0.75) also noted. Agent_08 assessment: the primary classification is correct; annotation adds necessary operational context. Value may change again within 2-4 weeks per source agent. Escalating given regime-change-adjacent nature of the update.

ESCALATION REQUIRED. Major US trade policy restructuring: SCOTUS 6-3 ruling upheld Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 as legal basis for emergency tariffs after striking down IEEPA tariff authority. 15% global tariff executive order now in full force effective Feb 24. 150-day limit expires approximately July 2026. Agent_07 confidence was 0.90; agent_08 cross-validation against SCOTUS opinion, White House Fact Sheet, Tax Foundation and Yale Budget Lab confirms the legal and policy change is real. Effective confidence raised to 0.98. Escalating because (1) magnitude is transformative for US trade policy, (2) the 150-day limit makes this a time-limited factor requiring tracking, (3) downstream effects on partner countries' tariff-related factors will require cascading updates in subsequent agents.

ESCALATION REQUIRED. US Treasury OFAC sanctions on Rwanda Defence Force and 4 senior commanders under DRC-related sanctions program. This is the first major US sanctions designation against a traditional African security partner. Agent_07 confidence was 0.93; source was single Treasury press release (sb0411). Agent_08 cross-validation: OFAC designation is official USG action — single official source is sufficient and authoritative (0.98 confidence after verification). Escalating because (1) binary field change from false to true is high-impact, (2) first-ever sanctions on Rwanda by USA, (3) bilateral relations factor for RWA-USA will require cascading update.

validation_notes: All 38 exchange rate updates (upd_001 to upd_038) passed plausibility and range checks. Previous values unavailable for delta analysis (first run with this schema version). No >30% weekly changes detected.

All 23 bond yield updates (upd_039 to upd_061) are within 0-50% range. Values are internally consistent with respective central bank policy rates. No >2pp changes flagged (no prior values available for comparison).

Equity data: KOSPI entries (upd_076, upd_077) flagged due to -7.24% daily move + circuit breaker event. DAX (upd_067, ACCEPT_WITH_NOTE) and Nikkei (upd_071, ACCEPT_WITH_NOTE) show large but explainable declines.
All 9 central bank rate updates (upd_101 to upd_109) confirmed unchanged vs W10 previous values. Zero delta on all CB rates.

Commodity entries: Brent (upd_090) and WTI (upd_091) flagged for >7% weekly moves, causally explained by Hormuz/Bab-el-Mandeb dual closure. Gold (upd_093) and Silver (upd_094) ACCEPT_WITH_NOTE for all-time and multi-decade highs respectively.
FLAG count: 4 entries (upd_076, upd_077, upd_090, upd_091). Summary and breakdown are consistent.
Two null-value entries (upd_085 TSX daily change, upd_089 JSE daily change) accepted as data gaps, not errors.

KOSPI -7.24% and DAX -3.54% on same day provide cross-market validation of Iran crisis severity — moves are directionally consistent across all global indices

Central bank rates (upd_101 to upd_109): agent_07 numbered these as starting at upd_101 but the commodity section covers upd_090 to upd_100; this means 11 commodity entries not 9, consistent with the task brief (upd_099 to upd_109 for commodities, then upd_101 to upd_109 for CB rates — the actual range in the data file was upd_090 to upd_100 for commodities and upd_101 to upd_109 for CB rates). Numbering is internally consistent with source file.
